# Compatibility Gate Evidence

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/compatibilitygate -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

Executable SSOT: [`compatibility-gate.riido.json`](compatibility-gate.riido.json).

Evidence artifact: `compatibility-gate-evidence`.

The daemon starts a provider only when detection, host provenance, policy, scheduling requirements, and runtime freshness agree.

## Gate Inputs

| Input | Owner | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| provider detection and capability fingerprint | C3 provider capability + C4 runtimeactor | `capability-contract`, `capability-fingerprint` |
| user-selected executable provenance | C11 hostintegration | `provenance-precedence` |
| store channel restrictions | C11 + C7 store channel policy | `store-channel-provider-path` |
| unsafe bypass and native config permissions | C7 policy bundle | `unsafe-bypass-policy`, `native-config-policy` |
| task runtime requirements | C5 scheduling | `scheduling-eligibility` |

## Gate Order

1. `resolve executable` - Resolve env override or PATH candidate before provider probing. (`override-pin`)
2. `fail closed on invalid override` - An explicit invalid override does not fall back to PATH. (`override-fail-closed-test`)
3. `allow OpenClaw adapter candidate selection` - Without an explicit override, OpenClaw may scan same-name PATH candidates until one satisfies the calendar-version gate. (`openclaw-candidate-scan`, `detected-executable-start`)
4. `detect provider capability` - Adapter detect normalizes availability and fingerprint inputs into the capability contract. (`capability-detect-start`, `capability-fingerprint`)
5. `apply host provenance` - Stronger provenance replaces weaker records, never the opposite. (`provenance-precedence`)
6. `apply policy gates` - Store, unsafe bypass, and native config policy decisions gate runtime use. (`store-channel-provider-path`, `unsafe-bypass-policy`, `native-config-policy`)
7. `select eligible runtime` - Scheduling chooses only compatible runtimes that satisfy task surfaces. (`scheduling-eligibility`, `scheduling-select`)
8. `block unavailable runtime` - Unavailable provider capabilities are projected as blocked before execution. (`runtime-blocked-unavailable`)

## Outputs

- provider-neutral compatibility status
- blocked/degraded reasons
- detected fingerprint metadata
- runtime status snapshots

## Failure Semantics

| Case | Meaning | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| missing optional provider binary | unavailable, not fatal to daemon startup | `runtime-blocked-unavailable` |
| misconfigured explicit path | unavailable with fail-closed reason | `override-fail-closed-test` |
| policy/store violation | blocked and not runnable | `store-channel-provider-path`, `unsafe-bypass-policy` |
| runtime fingerprint drift | handled by runtime upgrade flow | `capability-fingerprint` |

## Assertions

- `Compatibility order is generated from this manifest.`
- `Every gate input, step, and failure semantic points at source evidence.`
- `Reader docs must not be manually edited.`
